āmisānuppadānena.meaning: by providing food; by the giving of material things
āmisānuppadānena.gram: neuter compound action noun, instrumental singular
breakdown: Providing, furnishing, giving along (anu + pa + dāna).

ānayanapaccupaṭṭhāno.meaning: manifesting as escorting; whose manifestation is bringing along (a commentarial characterisation)
breakdown: Manifestation, appearance (commentarial term for how a quality presents itself).

ānesuṁ.gram: verb, 3rd person plural aorist of āneti (ā + √nī)
breakdown: Root: to lead. āneti: to bring.
Root: to lead. ā + √nī: to bring.

ānubhāvañca.meaning: and the dignity, the majesty; and the power
ānubhāvañca.gram: masculine abstract noun, accusative singular + enclitic ca
breakdown: Dignity, majesty, power (strengthened form of anubhāva, from anu + √bhū).

ānāpāna.meaning: in-breath and out-breath; the natural breath as the meditation object
breakdown: Out-breath, breathing out. Sandhi: āna + apāna > ānāpāna.
ānāpāna.goenka: Ānāpāna is the observation of the natural breath — the in-breath and out-breath just as it is — and in this tradition it is the foundational practice that precedes Vipassana. Klaus introduces ānāpānassati, mindfulness of the natural breath, with the canonical statement: “Ānāpānassati, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā” — mindfulness of breathing, when developed and fully engaged in, is of great fruit, of great benefit. He further notes that with the development and full engagement of ānāpānassati, the four satipaṭṭhānā get fulfilled. The breath is observed bare and natural, never controlled or regulated — it is not a breathing exercise.

ānītā.gram: past participle of āneti, feminine singular or masculine plural

āpadāsu.meaning: in misfortunes; in times of adversity
āpadāsu.gram: feminine noun, locative plural of āpadā
breakdown: Root: to go, fall. āpadā: misfortune, calamity.

āpajjanto.gram: present participle of āpajjati, masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Root: to go. āpajjati: to enter upon, undergo.

āpajjatī.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present of āpajjati (with metrically long final vowel)
breakdown: Root: to go. āpajjati: to get into, undergo, produce.
3rd person singular present ending (long -ī metri causa).

āpajjeyyaṁ.meaning: I should enter upon, I might undergo
āpajjeyyaṁ.gram: verb, 1st person singular optative of āpajjati
breakdown: Root: to go. āpajjati: to enter, undergo.

āpajjitabbaṁ.meaning: should be undertaken, to be produced; to be entered upon
āpajjitabbaṁ.gram: future passive participle of āpajjati, neuter singular
breakdown: Future passive participle suffix: “to be done”.

āpajjitā.gram: past participle of āpajjati, feminine singular or masculine plural

āpajjiṁsu.meaning: they produced, they got into, they made
āpajjiṁsu.gram: verb, 3rd person plural aorist of āpajjati
breakdown: Root: to go. āpajjati: to get into, produce.

āpanno.meaning: possessed of, entered upon; having incurred
āpanno.gram: past participle of āpajjati, masculine nominative singular

āpatti.meaning: offence, transgression (especially against the monastic rules)
breakdown: Root: to go, fall. āpatti: a falling into — an offence.

āpattianāpattiyā.meaning: of offence and non-offence; concerning being guilty and being free from guilt
breakdown: Non-offence, freedom from guilt (an- negative + āpatti).

āpātadaso.meaning: seeing what is within range; one who sees only what falls into view
breakdown: What comes into range or view.
Seeing (√dis). Together: seeing what is within the range.

āpāthamāgataṁ.meaning: come into the range (of a sense organ); entered the field of perception
āpāthamāgataṁ.gram: phrase: accusative noun + past participle (āpāthaṁ + āgataṁ)
breakdown: The sphere or range of a sense organ (accusative āpāthaṁ; the -m- is sandhi).
Come, arrived, entered (past participle of āgacchati).

ārabbha.meaning: starting, undertaking; with reference to, concerning
ārabbha.gram: gerund of ārabhati used adverbially (ā + √rabh + ya)
breakdown: Gerund ending: “having undertaken” — hence with reference to.

āraddhosmi.meaning: I am resolved; I am resolute
āraddhosmi.gram: sandhi fusion: past participle + 1st person singular of atthi (āraddho + asmi)
breakdown: Resolved, resolute; begun (past participle of ārabhati).

āraddhā.gram: past participle of ārabhati, feminine singular or masculine plural

ārakkhasārathi.meaning: a guard-charioteer; a driver serving as guard

ārammaṇābhiniropanalakkhaṇo.meaning: having the characteristic of fixing the mind upon the object (a commentarial definition of vitakka, applied thought)
breakdown: Fixation, application of the mind onto.

āratī.gram: feminine abstract noun (ārati, with metrically long final vowel), from āramati (ā + √ram)
breakdown: Root: to delight, stop. ārati: ceasing, abstinence.

ārocesi.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist of āroceti (ā + √ruc, causative)
breakdown: Root: to shine; causative roce: to make clear — to announce.

āropayitvāna.meaning: having made go up, having put on, having taken up to
breakdown: Root: to ascend; causative ropaya: to make ascend, put upon.

āropetvā.meaning: having put on, having got ready; having raised up
breakdown: Root: to ascend; causative rope: to make ascend, set upon.

ārācārī.meaning: living far from (evil); keeping remote — living a virtuous life

breakdown: Root: to succeed. ārādheti: to accomplish, win.

ārāmarāmaṇeyyakaṁ.meaning: the delightfulness of pleasure parks; enjoyable parkland

āsana.gram: neuter noun, from āsati (√ās, to sit)
breakdown: Noun suffix: that on which one sits — a seat.

āsanārahassa.meaning: of one worthy of a seat; for those who deserve a seat

āsavānaṁ.meaning: of the āsavas — literally “that which flows”: the mental intoxicants, the impurities that intoxicate the mind
āsavānaṁ.gram: masculine noun, genitive plural of āsava (ā + √su, to flow)
breakdown: Root: to flow. āsava: outflow — what intoxicates the mind, deep impurity.
āsavānaṁ.goenka: The āsavas are the deep mental intoxicants or taints — literally “that which flows”, what oozes out and intoxicates the mind. In this tradition the destruction of the āsavas marks full liberation: the khīṇāsavo is the Arahant whose mind is freed from all four — kāmāsava (desire for sensuality), bhavāsava (desire for living), diṭṭhāsava (attachment to philosophical speculations) and avijjāsava (ignorance).

breakdown: Root: to expect, hope. āsaṁsā: expectation.
Adjectival suffix: given to, full of.

āsevati to.summary: A glossary fragment; the entry is simply āsevati, “to frequent, visit”.

āsiṁ.gram: verb, 1st person singular aorist of atthi (√as, to be)

āsāduppajahavaggo,.meaning: the Āsāduppajaha Vagga (“Chapter on Hopes Hard to Abandon”), in the Tatiyapaṇṇāsaka of the Dukanipāta, Aṅguttaranikāya

ātappamanvāya.meaning: in consequence of ardour; following upon exertion
breakdown: Ardour, exertion, zeal (accusative ātappaṁ; the -m- is sandhi).
Following upon, in consequence of (gerund of anu + √i).

āvaraṇīyehi.gram: future passive participle of āvareti used as noun, instrumental plural
breakdown: Future passive participle suffix: what obstructs.

āvasathāgāraṁ.meaning: a rest-house; a dwelling-hall, council hall
breakdown: House, hall (sandhi lengthens the joining vowel: -tha + a- > -thā-).

āvikattā.meaning: one who manifests, shows, explains; a revealer
breakdown: Maker, doer (agent noun of karoti). Together: one who makes manifest.

āvikatvā.meaning: having made clear, having explained; having revealed
breakdown: Having made (gerund of karoti). Together: having made manifest.
Root: to fight (yudh > vudh). āvudha: instrument of fighting — a weapon.
